title: Architectural constraint: no AI impersonation ever
summary: K507 was built under the hard constraint in feedback_no_ai_impersonation_ever.md (B124): if a task requires another vendor, route through their actual API or decline. Path C (Bishop emulation) was explicitly rejected because it requires trusting Bishop's word, which has been violated before. K507 ships Path B.

title: Perplexity Sonar Pro API shape verified
summary: Single verification call confirmed: model='sonar-pro', response.id is a UUID (verifiable request ID), usage.prompt_tokens and usage.completion_tokens are present, usage.cost.total_cost is the machine-reported cost per call (not a calculated estimate), citations[] is a list of URLs. HTTP 200 = valid auth. HTTP 401 = bad key. HTTP 429 = rate limit with x-ratelimit-reset-after header.

title: Perplexity API pricing and cost field
summary: Sonar Pro API returns usage.cost.total_cost per call. Empirical: 9 input tokens → input_tokens_cost=$0.00003, so input pricing ≈$3/1M. request_cost=$0.006 per call (note: Perplexity charges per-request fee on Sonar Pro, ~$0.005-0.006). pawn_with_substrate.py uses the API-reported cost as authoritative when present, falls back to SONAR_PRO_PRICING_INPUT=3.00, SONAR_PRO_PRICING_OUTPUT=15.00, SONAR_PRO_REQUEST_COST=0.005.

title: Intent classification: 7-category keyword-based router
summary: pawn_with_substrate.py classifies queries into 7 intents: canonical, outreach, architecture, founder_voice, benchmark, operational, default. Classification is keyword-scoring: count keyword hits per intent, pick the highest-scoring. All 5 E1 test queries routed correctly. Override via --intent flag.

title: Substrate context builder: 7 intent blocks + canonical base
summary: Substrate = SUBSTRATE_INTRO + SUBSTRATE_CANONICAL (always included, with live canonical numbers from overview.json) + intent-specific block. Canonical block includes 83.3% creator keep, $5/yr membership, Cost+20% margin, innovation count, crown jewels, patent applications, formal claims, production systems. Intent blocks add domain-specific context (outreach=crown recipients + letter system; architecture=Cathedral Effect + Trust Match + Librarian MCP; etc).

title: Substrate injection: estimated 178-356 tokens depending on intent
summary: Substrate context sizes by intent: canonical=178 est tokens (987 chars), outreach=300 est tokens (1674 chars), architecture=356 est tokens (2038 chars), founder_voice=338 est tokens (1831 chars), benchmark=318 est tokens (1853 chars). Overhead is logged in substrate_savings_log.jsonl per call. The canonical block is always included (adds ~120 tokens base).

title: Savings telemetry: verified_real_call and via_api flags
summary: K507 API calls log to substrate_savings_log.jsonl with verified_real_call=True (distinguishes from any future estimated-only entries) and via_api=True (distinguishes from manual browser-paste entries logged by pawn_session_savings.py). The pawn_request_id field contains the Perplexity request UUID — independently verifiable in Perplexity's API dashboard. First K507 log entry confirmed: request_id=ee460697-65b6-46fc-b56d-f0e6cc27d8e8.

title: Error handling: structured errors, never fabricated responses
summary: Connection error → RuntimeError with Perplexity status URL. Timeout (60s) → RuntimeError. HTTP 401 → clear auth failure message, reference to SDS.env. HTTP 429 → backoff (min of reset header or 60s) + single retry, then structured error including rate-limit reset time. HTTP 5xx → structured error with Perplexity status URL. Non-JSON response → error. In all cases: no fabricated response, non-zero exit code.

title: pyproject.toml: pawn-substrate console script entry
summary: Created librarian-mcp/pyproject.toml with package name librarian-mcp, version 0.2.0. Defines three console scripts: pawn-substrate (K507), knight-savings (K505), pawn-savings (K505). Package-dir maps 'librarian_mcp_scripts' → scripts/ directory. Install with: pip install -e librarian-mcp/. After install, pawn-substrate is available globally as the Developer-tier CLI bonus.

title: LB Test Frame options.html: Developer — Pawn API Configuration section
summary: Added Developer-tier section to lb-test-frame/extension/pages/options.html. Section is hidden for Casual persona, shown for Developer and Member. Includes: password field for Perplexity API key (stored in chrome.storage.local, never sent to LB servers), Test key button (makes live API verification call from browser, reports model/request_id/tokens), daily cost cap field, CLI install path field. JavaScript testPawnAPI() confirms key validity via direct browser→Perplexity API call.

title: E1 intent-routing verification: 5/5 canonical queries routed correctly
summary: Test results: (1) 'What is the LB membership cost?' → canonical PASS; (2) 'Why is the AAAI op-ed important?' → outreach PASS; (3) 'How does Trust Match work?' → architecture PASS; (4) 'What does the Founder think about AI impersonation?' → founder_voice PASS; (5) 'What is the R13 cross-vendor lift?' → benchmark PASS. All substrate blocks built successfully. No substrate injection failures.

title: E2 verifiable-call audit: canonical query end-to-end confirmed
summary: Full E2 audit: query='What is the LB membership cost and what does a creator keep on a $500 transaction?' → intent=canonical → substrate injected (178 tokens) → real Perplexity API call → model=sonar-pro, request_id=ee460697-65b6-46fc-b56d-f0e6cc27d8e8. Answer correctly stated $5/yr, 83.3%, $416.67, Cost+20%. Saved to savings log with verified_real_call=True. Cost: $0.00799 for 278 input / 77 output tokens. All K507 structural properties confirmed.

title: PowerShell wrapper: pawn_with_substrate.ps1
summary: Thin PowerShell wrapper at librarian-mcp/scripts/pawn_with_substrate.ps1. Loads PERPLEXITY_API_KEY from SDS.env (via safe pattern, no echo), then delegates all args to pawn_with_substrate.py. Supports -Query, -Intent, -Test, -Summary, -CostDay, -NoLog, -Raw, -Model, -TaskLabel parameters. Windows-native invocation pattern for Knight's machine.

title: Cost projection: Sonar Pro at typical Founder usage
summary: At 10 queries/day with avg 2000 input + 500 output tokens per query: daily=$0.1038, monthly=$3.11, annual=$37.89. At the empirically observed cost ($0.00799/canonical query with 278/77 tokens): 10 queries/day → $0.08/day → $29.17/yr. Actual rate will be measured empirically after K507 accumulates sessions. Budget cap: $5.00 for K507 session (Perplexity API verification calls ~$0.50 as projected in prompt).
